body: I have obtained mercy to be somebody, if I shall attain to God.

title: Papias of Hierapolis
body: A share is given to all by the Father, according as each one is or shall be worthy.

title: Polycarp of Smyrna
body: He has set us this example in Himself, and we have believed that such is the case.

title: Justin Martyr
body: Those out of all the nations who are pious and righteous through the faith of Christ, look for His future appearance.

title: Theophilus of Antioch
body: For if you love them who love you, what reward will you have?

title: Irenaeus of Lyons
body: The love of God, being rich and ungrudging, confers upon the suppliant more than he can ask from it.

title: Hippolytus of Rome
body: He comes from heaven with power, and angels, and the glory of His Father.

title: Dionysius of Alexandria
body: They are not remembered for these, but only for what they may have evinced of wisdom or folly, virtue or vice.

title: Cyprian of Carthage
body: She spoke with hidden prayer, but with manifest faith.

title: Gregory Thaumaturgus
body: Now the Lord is that Spirit; and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.

title: Dionysius of Rome
body: Christ is the Word, the Wisdom, and the Power.

title: Victorinus of Pettau
body: This Word, when it made light, is called Wisdom.

title: Methodius of Olympus
body: The Gentiles shall come to your light, and kings to the brightness of your rising.

title: Pamphilus
body: Then the thanksgivings offered up by the Church for the faithful constancy of the apostles.

title: Peter of Alexandria
body: Both things are demonstrated, that He was God by nature, and that He was man by nature.

title: Alexander of Alexandria
body: He who denies that the brightness of the glory existed, takes away also the primitive light of which it is the brightness.

title: Athanasius of Alexandria
body: In truth He is the Son of God, for men are all subject to death.

title: Hilary of Poitiers
body: May my lot be no useless strife of words, but the unwavering confession of an unhesitating faith!

title: Ephrem the Syrian
body: O Lord, increase our peace, that we may keep three feasts of the Godhead.

title: Cyril of Jerusalem
body: There is a second kind of faith, which is bestowed by Christ as a gift of grace.

title: Basil the Great
body: In truth the most difficult of sciences is to know one's self.

title: Gregory Nazianzen
body: God is to intelligible things what the sun is to the things of sense.

title: Gregory of Nyssa
body: For our Lord has announced that the life after our resurrection shall be as that of the angels.

title: Ambrose of Milan
body: Open your breast to a friend that he may be faithful to you, and that you may receive the delight of life.

title: Jerome
body: The truth is that the saints are not called dead, but are said to be asleep.

title: John Chrysostom
body: Let us hold fast to Mercy: she is the schoolmistress and teacher of that higher Wisdom.

title: Augustine of Hippo
body: This is the grace of God making us good; this is His mercy preventing us.

title: John Cassian
body: A lasting joy will remove them from servile fear and mercenary hope to the love of God.

title: Vincent of Lerins
body: Let no license be allowed to novelty, since it is not fit that any addition be made to antiquity.

title: Leo the Great
body: Follow after humility which the Son of God deigned to teach His disciples.

title: Gregory the Great
body: The mind that is bound by the bondage of fear knows not the grace of liberty.

title: John of Damascus
body: Let us give honour to her who bore God as being strictly and truly the Mother of God.

title: Clement of Rome
body: By Him are the eyes of our hearts opened.

title: Ignatius of Antioch
body: Have a regard to preserve unity, than which nothing is better.

title: Papias of Hierapolis
body: They ascend through the Spirit to the Son, and through the Son to the Father.

title: Polycarp of Smyrna
body: He taught the word of truth accurately and steadfastly in the presence of those then alive.

title: Justin Martyr
body: The Lord shall inherit Judah his portion in the holy land, and He shall choose Jerusalem again.

title: Theophilus of Antioch
body: Since you said, Show me your God, this is my God, and I counsel you to fear Him and trust Him.

title: Irenaeus of Lyons
body: Shun evil, and do good: seek peace, and pursue it.

title: Hippolytus of Rome
body: The Lord considered and knew those that were His, and drew His saints to Himself.

title: Dionysius of Alexandria
body: The memory of the just is blessed, but the name of the wicked is extinguished.

title: Cyprian of Carthage
body: If we are fellow-heirs with Christ, let us abide in the peace of Christ; if we are sons of God, let us be peacemakers.

title: Gregory Thaumaturgus
body: He who in the fear of God submits himself, escapes all that is contrary.

title: Dionysius of Rome
body: To make and to create are different from one another.

title: Victorinus of Pettau
body: John the evangelist, like an eagle on uplifted wings, speaks of the Word of God.

title: Methodius of Olympus
body: So do they truly believe who hold fast to the pure faith.

title: Pamphilus
body: The unity of spirit pervading the believers and promoting the common good.

title: Peter of Alexandria
body: Christ our Passover was sacrificed for us.

title: Alexander of Alexandria
body: There is no communion between light and darkness, no concord between Christ and Belial.

title: Athanasius of Alexandria
body: The godly teaching and the faith according to Christ brands their foolish language as godlessness.

title: Hilary of Poitiers
body: In devout thoughts such as these my soul, engrossed in the pursuit of truth, took its delight.

title: Ephrem the Syrian
body: A treasure of helpful graces is this day, for Light gleamed forth upon our blindness!

title: Cyril of Jerusalem
body: Christ the Lord is He who was born in the city of David.

title: Basil the Great
body: Be like a green olive tree in the house of God, decked through faith with the bloom of salvation.

title: Gregory Nazianzen
body: Approach the truth: be converted at this late hour.

title: Gregory of Nyssa
body: I ask for all regard for the truth: and we ourselves will give evidence.

title: Ambrose of Milan
body: Let love of money be destroyed, let lust die.

title: Jerome
body: We are dead, and our life is hid with Christ in God.

title: John Chrysostom
body: Let us abide in the peace and in the grace of God.

title: Augustine of Hippo
body: Let charity be in you, and let it rejoice in the truth.

title: John Cassian
body: Where those of our own household are not opposed to us, there also the kingdom of God is secured in peace of heart.

title: Vincent of Lerins
body: If novelty is to be shunned, antiquity is to be held fast; if novelty is profane, antiquity is sacred.

title: Leo the Great
body: But this godly love cannot be perfect unless a man love his neighbour also.

title: Gregory the Great
body: He who has no care to keep peace refuses to bear the fruit of the Spirit.

title: John of Damascus
body: By the grace of God they are maintained and preserved.

title: Clement of Rome
body: How blessed and wonderful, beloved, are the gifts of God!

title: Ignatius of Antioch
body: If these things were done by our Lord only in appearance, then am I also only in appearance bound.

title: Papias of Hierapolis
body: The just man who is on the earth shall forget to die.

title: Polycarp of Smyrna
body: We do not commend those who give themselves up, seeing the Gospel does not teach so to do.

title: Justin Martyr
body: God said to him, Your name shall be no more called Jacob, but Israel shall he your name.

title: Theophilus of Antioch
body: God caused to spring out of the earth every tree beautiful in appearance, or good for food.

title: Irenaeus of Lyons
body: My salvation shall be for ever, and my righteousness shall not pass away.

title: Hippolytus of Rome
body: He alone has power of life and death.

title: Dionysius of Alexandria
body: Show unto you that eternal life which was with the Father, and was manifested unto us.

title: Cyprian of Carthage
body: He who has not charity has not God.

title: Gregory Thaumaturgus
body: She shall bring forth a son, and they shall call His name Jesus: for He shall save His people from their sins.

title: Dionysius of Rome
body: We must believe in God the Father Omnipotent, and on Christ Jesus His Son, and on the Holy Spirit.

title: Victorinus of Pettau
body: When for man's salvation He was made man to overcome death.

title: Methodius of Olympus
body: Death belongs not to it; a story of salvation is our song.

title: Pamphilus
body: Paul's exhortation to those with him as to his hope of deliverance.

title: Peter of Alexandria
body: He Himself, as the true Lamb, was sacrificed for us in the feast of the typical Passover.

title: Alexander of Alexandria
body: If the image of God was not always, it is clear also that He was not always, of which it is the image.

title: Athanasius of Alexandria
body: The Lord is both Wisdom and Truth, and is not in the second place after another wisdom.

title: Hilary of Poitiers
body: As the Way, He will guide us to the Truth; the Truth will establish us in the Life.

title: Ephrem the Syrian
body: Your bowels of mercy upon Your day make abundant to us, O Lord!

title: Cyril of Jerusalem
body: Knowing this, therefore, let us walk spiritually, that we may be counted worthy of God's adoption.

title: Basil the Great
body: Again He is called royal, Spirit of truth, and Spirit of wisdom.

title: Gregory Nazianzen
body: Christ is freed from the tomb, be freed from the bond of sin.

title: Gregory of Nyssa
body: The eye enjoys the light by virtue of having light within itself to seize its kindred light.

title: Ambrose of Milan
body: Let no persecution lessen your love, which many waters cannot quench, nor many rivers drown.

title: Jerome
body: Christ has reconciled us in His body to God the Father through His death.

title: John Chrysostom
body: Be a lover then of love; for this is the thing that preserves all good things.

title: Augustine of Hippo
body: My faith calls on You, which You have breathed into me through the incarnation of Your Son.

title: John Cassian
body: In the Incarnation of Your Only Begotten Son they may perceive both Your gift and His love.

title: Vincent of Lerins
body: They who had not ventured to tamper with it proved themselves Confessors and martyrs.

title: Leo the Great
body: He Who is true God, is also true man: and there is no lie in either nature.

title: Gregory the Great
body: There is nothing safer for defense than sincerity, nothing easier to say than truth.

title: John of Damascus
body: The Lord's flesh is life-giving spirit because it was conceived of the life-giving Spirit.

title: Clement of Rome
body: Let us attend to what is good, pleasing, and acceptable in the sight of Him who formed us.

title: Ignatius of Antioch
body: Be watchful, possessing a sleepless spirit.
